title: Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory
text:
Chemical biology provides a unique approach to study biological systems by using synthetic small molecules. Unlike common biological methods involving genetic engineering and immunochemistry, small-molecule chemical tools can be delivered easily and modified readily with high specificity, providing versatile and adaptable methods for biological investigation.

Our research goal is to develop and apply small-molecule tools to identify and characterize proteases and metal ions involved in mitochondrial physiology, and to expand these tools toward biomarker discovery and drug target identification for Alzheimer’s disease and cancers.

title: Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory

title: Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory
text:
Lab Updates

2026.06.30
Website redesign in progress.
The Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory website is being redesigned using Quarto and GitHub Pages.

title: Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory
text:
Research Areas









Activity-Based Probes
Molecular Biomarker Discovery and Fluorescent Probe Development
Peptidomimetic Transporters


We develop activity-based probes to monitor enzyme function in cells, animals, and disease models, with particular interest in proteases relevant to neurodegenerative diseases.
We design chemical tools and fluorescent probes to detect disease-associated molecular activities and support biomarker discovery.
We develop peptoid and peptidomimetic scaffolds for organelle targeting and intracellular delivery of biologically active cargos.

title: Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory
text: We welcome motivated students and researchers interested in chemical biology, medicinal chemistry, molecular probes, drug target discovery, biomarker identification, and disease-relevant small-molecule tools.

title: Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory
text:
Research Interests
Prospective students and collaborators are encouraged to contact us if they are interested in one or more of the following areas:

Drug target discovery and target validation
Biomarker discovery and fluorescence-based detection
Chemical biology and molecular probe development
Protease biology and activity-based probes
Peptidomimetic transporters and intracellular delivery
AI-assisted peptide and protein binder design

title: Drug Target and Biomarker Discovery Laboratory
text:
Chemical probes are key players in searching for the potential druggability of new molecular targets, pathways, or biological processes. Particularly, pathfinder molecules in drug discovery projects aid the design and evaluation of biological assays and the identification of useful biomarkers.
Here are a few examples of chemical tools that our lab has primarily focused on studying.



Activity-based probes react with specific classes of enzymes in a manner that correlates with enzyme activity, enabling the monitoring of enzyme function and activity in cells, animals, and disease models.
Our lab aims to develop activity-based probes for various proteases to study the pathogenesis of neurodegenerative diseases.








Recent advances in gene therapy and antibody drug development have brought renewed attention to molecular transporters as efficient delivery systems. Peptoids have unique advantages over other polymeric delivery scaffolds because they share physiological and chemical properties with peptides and proteins while offering superior metabolic and immunological stability.








We design chemical tools and fluorescent probes to detect disease-associated molecular activities and support biomarker discovery.
